videoTitle: Best AI Code Review Tools for Azure DevOps (2026) | Qodo Academy
videoDescription:
🔗 Try Qodo for free → https://qodo.ai

This chapter of Qodo’s AI Code Review Academy explains how to choose an AI code review tool for Azure DevOps in 2026 — and why most GitHub-focused comparison lists do not hold up once you look at Azure Repos, Azure Pipelines, Azure Boards, Azure DevOps Server, and air-gapped deployment.

“Supports Azure DevOps” can mean a simple webhook comment, or it can mean a native review experience that works inside pull requests, runs through pipelines, links findings to work items, and can be deployed on your own infrastructure.

This video focuses on the criteria that actually matter:

1. Native Azure Repos comments
2. Azure Pipelines integration
3. Azure Boards work item linking
4. Azure DevOps Server support
5. Air-gapped and on-premises deployment
6. Cross-repository analysis
7. Enforceable rules learned from review history

Qodo is the dedicated AI review platform in this comparison. GitHub Copilot Code Review has a more limited Azure DevOps experience than it does on GitHub, while Greptile and Graphite do not support Azure DevOps at all.

The biggest differences appear where enterprise Azure DevOps teams actually operate: self-hosted environments, regulated infrastructure, cross-project dependencies, and coding standards that need to be enforced consistently.

The video shows Qodo reviewing an API server pull request that looks safe inside its own repository but would break a dependent web client. Qodo detects the cross-repository conflict, explains the downstream impact, and surfaces the issue before merge.

It also covers Rule Miner, which learns from accepted review comments in your Azure DevOps history and turns recurring team decisions into enforceable rules. That means standards do not have to stay trapped in senior reviewers’ heads or old pull request threads.

For Azure DevOps Server, deployment can decide the shortlist immediately. If your code must stay on-premises, run in a private cloud, or operate with no outbound internet, most cloud-only reviewers are ruled out — while Qodo remains an option.

📚 Full AI Code Review Academy → https://www.qodo.ai/academy

⏱️ Chapters:

0:00 Azure DevOps Changes the Shortlist
0:45 What “Support” Should Actually Mean
1:22 Which Code Review Tools Really Fit Azure DevOps in 2026
2:43 Rule Miner Turns History Into Standards
3:33 Qodo Catches a Cross-Repo Breakage
5:29 Why Server and Air-Gapped Matter
6:30 Code Review Tools Comparison by Team Type

💡 Key takeaways:

→ Native Azure Repos comments, pipelines, Boards linking, and Server support matter more than a generic “Azure DevOps supported” badge

→ Qodo is built for deeper Azure DevOps integration, cross-repository reasoning, enforceable standards, and enterprise deployment

→ Rule Miner converts accepted review patterns into rules your team can apply automatically

→ Cross-project context catches bugs that look harmless inside one repository but break another service

→ Azure DevOps Server, private cloud, and air-gapped requirements eliminate most cloud-only tools

→ AI review complements tools like GitHub Advanced Security, Snyk, and SonarQube rather than replacing them

🧠 About Qodo

Qodo is the AI code review platform built for engineering teams that ship at scale. It analyzes full codebase context, enforces team-specific standards through a living rules system, and catches critical issues, breaking changes, and duplicated logic before merge.

videoTitle: Episodio 5: Tu dashboard está verde. Tu agente está mintiendo.
videoDescription:
CPU baja. Memoria estable. `HTTP 200 OK` en todos los endpoints.

Todo perfecto. Y mientras tanto, tu agente le está diciendo a un usuario que tu producto es gratuito.

Las métricas tradicionales de DevOps monitorizan la salud del sistema. No la salud semántica. Un agente puede fallar de forma catastrófica mientras su infraestructura está perfectamente sana.

La observabilidad de agentes requiere una capa diferente: rastrear el camino de ejecución multi-paso, el uso de tokens, la precisión de recuperación RAG, la latencia de llamadas a herramientas. No solo registras errores — registras *procesos de pensamiento*.

Si tratas a tus agentes como aplicaciones estándar, estás volando a ciegas.

En este último vídeo de la serie te explico por qué necesitas una caja negra de MLOps para tus agentes.
channelDescription:
Los negocios se están convirtiendo en compañías de software y DevOps permite incrementar cientos de veces la frecuencia de entrega de cambios de software, aumentando la competitividad de la empresa, la adaptación de su portfolio de productos y reducir su tiempo de comercialización.

En Minuto DevOps te presentaré los conceptos clave para entender qué es el DevOps.

videoTitle: FlexDeploy from Flexagon: Simplify Salesforce DevOps From Code to Deployment
videoDescription:
Managing Salesforce DevOps doesn't have to mean juggling unfamiliar tools and guessing at project health. This demo walks through how FlexDeploy brings governance, visibility, and automation to every stage of the Salesforce development lifecycle.
See how FlexDeploy supports No-Code, Low-Code, and pro-code development, tracks work with a built-in Issue Tracking System, and compares Orgs by object, object type, or full repository sync. Explore package management, flexible Apex test levels, automated detection of Profiles and Test Classes, and release pipelines with built-in governance, approvals, and role-based access control. Plus, see FlexDeploy's Continuous Integration triggers, environment-specific Replacements, and dashboards that turn your DevOps data into actionable insights.
Learn more at flexagon.com.
channelDescription:
Flexagon is an innovative software company focused on enabling enterprise agility and operational excellence through the adoption of software such as Flexagon's FlexDeploy and Oracle Fusion Middleware. 

FlexDeploy is our DevOps and Application Release Automation (ARA) product which standardizes and automates the lifecycle for building, packaging, testing, and deploying software across environments. 

More information at Flexagon.com

videoTitle: Infrastructure Abstraction Layers Tutorial | Kubernetes, IaC & Platform Engineering
videoDescription:

In this video, you'll learn Infrastructure Abstraction Layers in a simple and practical way with real-world examples.

🚀 What you'll learn:

✅ What are Infrastructure Abstraction Layers?
✅ Why modern DevOps teams use abstraction
✅ Physical Infrastructure vs Virtual Machines
✅ Containers & Kubernetes
✅ Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
✅ Platform Engineering
✅ Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs)
✅ Cloud Infrastructure abstraction
✅ Real-world deployment workflow
✅ Benefits for developers and organizations

You'll also discover how Infrastructure Abstraction Layers simplify deployments, improve developer productivity, reduce operational complexity, and help organizations build scalable cloud-native applications.

videoTitle: Linux for DevOps — Absolute vs Relative Paths: Navigate Any Linux System | Zero to LLMOps (Day 0D)
videoDescription:
Day 0D of a free 180-day DevOps course. This is the single idea that unblocks everything else — how to find your way around a Linux system from anywhere.

• Absolute paths — the full address from root (/), reliable from anywhere
• Relative paths — directions from where you're standing right now (pwd)
• The four shortcuts: . (here) · .. (up one) · ~ (home) · - (last dir)
• Getting lost on purpose — and always finding your way back with pwd and cd ~

⏱ Chapters
0:00 The most important idea
0:32 Recall from Day 0C
1:33 Absolute paths
2:13 Relative paths
3:07 The four shortcuts: . .. ~ -
3:46 Prove it live: absolute vs relative
5:33 Move around on purpose
7:23 Get lost, then get found
8:24 Recap & homework

Homework: pick 5 places on your machine and navigate to each two ways — once absolute, once relative.

◀ Previous — Day 0C  ·  ▶ Next — Day 0E
🔔 Subscribe for the full path from zero to LLMOps.
channelDescription:
Zero to LLMOps is a free, structured 180-day DevOps course — starting from “what is an operating system?” and building all the way to Bash, Git, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, the big three clouds, SRE, and finally MLOps & LLMOps pipelines.

No prior experience needed. Every session is taught from the terminal up, with hands-on labs and homework you can follow along with. New episodes regularly.

▶ Start at Day 0A. Then follow the Foundations playlist in order.

videoTitle: GreenOps Strategy Tutorial | AI for Sustainable Cloud Computing & Cost Optimization

videoTitle: What is SDLC? Software Development Life Cycle Explained | DevOps Complete Course 2026 | Episode 3
videoDescription:
🚀 Welcome to Episode 3 of the DevOps Masterclass 2026!

Before learning Docker, Jenkins, AWS, or CI/CD, it's important to understand how software is actually built in real companies.

In this video, you'll learn the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) using a real Employee Management System project that we'll continue building throughout this playlist.

📌 In this Episode:
✅ What is SDLC?
✅ Why Companies Follow SDLC
✅ 7 Phases of SDLC
✅ Requirement Gathering
✅ Planning
✅ Design
✅ Development
✅ Testing
✅ Deployment
✅ Maintenance

🎯 Real Project:
Employee Management System

Throughout this DevOps series, we'll use the same project to understand every concept practically instead of learning isolated tools.

📚 Complete Playlist Covers:

✔ SDLC
✔ Agile & Scrum

✔ Networking
✔ Git & GitHub
✔ Docker
✔ AWS
✔ Jenkins

✔ Monitoring

videoTitle: Linux for DevOps — Everything Is a File: The Linux Filesystem Tree | Zero to LLMOps (Day 0C)
videoDescription:
Day 0C of a free 180-day DevOps course. One idea unlocks Linux: everything is a file — and every file hangs off one single tree starting at /.

• "Everything is a file" — why devices and kernel info are files too
• One root, one tree: /home /etc /var /tmp /usr /opt /bin
• Make things for real: mkdir, touch, ls -l, cat
• Writing to a file with echo (output redirection)
• rm — the command with no undo, and how to stay safe with it

⏱ Chapters
0:00 Intro & recap of Day 0B
2:49 Everything is a file
3:31 One tree, one root (/)
3:51 The important directories
6:44 Make files: mkdir, touch, ls -l, cat
10:15 Writing with echo redirection
11:20 rm & staying safe
11:55 Recap & homework

Homework: build a folder tree 2 levels deep, 3 files, ready to ls -l through.

🔔 Subscribe for the full path from zero to LLMOps.
channelDescription:
Zero to LLMOps is a free, structured 180-day DevOps course — starting from “what is an operating system?” and building all the way to Bash, Git, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, the big three clouds, SRE, and finally MLOps & LLMOps pipelines.

No prior experience needed. Every session is taught from the terminal up, with hands-on labs and homework you can follow along with. New episodes regularly.

▶ Start at Day 0A. Then follow the Foundations playlist in order.
